title: What Scripture Actually Means by "Two Are Better Than One"
caption:
Ecclesiastes 4 gets read at so many weddings that men assume it's about a wife. Read the actual lines: if one falls, the other lifts him up. Woe to the one who falls with no one to help him up. That's not romance. That's a warning about being alone.

A cord of three strands is not quickly broken, Solomon writes. He's describing a rope built to hold weight under strain, not a feeling. A man alone is a single strand, and he snaps exactly where you'd expect.

The verse assumes you'll fall. Not if, when. What it promises isn't rescue from falling, it's someone close enough to reach you when you do.

The men who fall with no one to help them up usually didn't lose their brothers in the fall. They lost them years earlier, one skipped call at a time.

Who's your one or two? Save this and text him today, before you need him.

#faith #christianmen #brotherhood #scripture #godlymen #manofgod #christianity
